reasoning: The question resolves YES if there is another single-day drop of >5% in the S&P 500 between April 5 and December 31, 2025. The knowledge cutoff is November 9, 2025. Search results indicate that a massive 10.5% two-day drop occurred on April 3-4, 2025, prompting the creation of this market. However, there is no evidence of a subsequent 5% single-day drop occurring between April 5 and November 9 (mentions of specific drops in July only point to an individual stock like Tesla dropping >5%, whereas the broader market was making record highs). This suggests the event has not resolved YES yet. With only about 35 trading days left in the year and no recent evidence of extreme indices-wide single-day crash volatility on the scale of >5%, the likelihood of such a black swan event occurring before year-end is very low. A base rate estimate for a random 1.5 month period would be around 1-3%, but factoring in the potential for ongoing geopolitical/macro uncertainty, assigning a 7% probability appropriately reflects this tail risk while remaining realistic.
batch_queries: Will the S&P 500 AGAIN decrease by over 5% in one day in 2025?
